body.title: Consequence check: queue black-box + dispatch model
body.result: CLEAR — cmd_next marks in_progress at dispatch; direct CC sprints using setstatus work correctly; no edge cases with double-claim or missed in_progress transitions observed in practice
body.description:
**What changed:** The queue became an encapsulated black box — cmd_next marks in_progress at dispatch, no separate claim, date gates enforced by _gate_clear.
**Predicted unintended effects:** (1) direct CC sprints that relied on claim may silently skip the in_progress mark if verify logic has an edge case; (2) date-format gate fix may over-block tickets whose gate intentionally references both a date and a ticket ID; (3) worker_daemon may double-dispatch a ticket if cmd_next is called twice before the first in_progress write commits.
**Signals to watch:** channel log — any 'sprint timeout' on already-closed tickets (should be zero); cc_queue.py show on any gated ticket showing correct blocked state; no 'T-xxx already in_progress' errors from worker_daemon.
**Gate condition:** Check 14 days after T-dispatch-is-assignment closes.

**Affected files:** None — observation only
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** Observe; annotate as occurred / clear / partial and close
**Test plan:** no tests — observation ticket
